requirements.full_sentence:
The CFS shall be prepared using uniform accounting policies for like transactions
and other events in similar circumstances.
requirements.object: accounting policies for like transactions and other events in similar circumstances

conditions: If it is not practicable to do so
requirements.full_sentence:
If it is not practicable to do so, that fact
shall be disclosed together with the proportions of the items in the CFS to which
the different accounting policies have been applied.
requirements.object: the proportions of the items in the CFS to which the different accounting policies have been applied

requirements.full_sentence:
For preparing the CFS using
uniform accounting policies, an AIFI shall rely on a Statement of Adjustments for
non-uniform accounting policies, furnished by the Statutory Auditors of the
subsidiaries.
requirements.object: of Adjustments for non-uniform accounting policies, furnished by the Statutory Auditors of the subsidiaries

requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where different entities in a group are governed by different accounting
norms laid down by the concerned regulator for different businesses, for
consolidation purposes, an AIFI shall use the rules and regulatory requirements
applicable to AIFIs in respect of like transactions and other events in similar
circumstances.
requirements.object: regulatory requirements applicable to AIFIs in respect of like transactions and other events in similar circumstances

conditions:
where regulatory norms have been prescribed by the
Reserve Bank
requirements.full_sentence:
In situations where regulatory norms have been prescribed by the
Reserve Bank, the norms as applicable according to the accounting standards
may be followed.

For valuation, the investments in associates (other than those specifically
excluded under AS 23) shall be accounted for under the 'Equity Method' of
accounting in accordance with AS 23.
requirements.object: the 'Equity Method' of accounting in accordance with AS 23

requirements.full_sentence:
The valuation of investments in subsidiaries which are not consolidated and
associates which are excluded under AS 23, shall be as per the relevant
<image>
valuation norms issued by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: relevant <image> valuation norms issued by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The valuation of investments in
joint ventures shall be accounted for under the 'proportionate consolidation'
method as per AS 27.
requirements.object: the 'proportionate consolidation' method as per AS 27

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI may take into account the provisions of the
accounting standards relating to the exclusion of subsidiaries, associates or joint
ventures from consolidation under specific circumstances.
requirements.object: provisions of the accounting standards relating to the exclusion of subsidiaries, associates or joint ventures from consolidatio

requirements.full_sentence:
The solo level financial statements and the CFS shall be submitted to the
Department of Supervision, Reserve Bank of India within one month from the
publication of an AIFI's annual accounts.
requirements.object: Department of Supervision, Reserve Bank of India within one month from the publication of an AIFI's annual accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall be guided by following with respect to relevant issues in the
application of certain Accounting Standards.
requirements.object: with respect to relevant issues in the application of certain Accounting Standards

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Non-recognition of income by an AIFI in the case of non-performing
advances and non-performing investments, in compliance with the
regulatory prescriptions of the Reserve Bank, should not attract a
qualification by the statutory auditors as this would be in conformity with
provisions of the standard since it recognises postponement of recognition
of revenue where collectability of the revenue is significantly uncertain.
requirements.object: the statutory auditors as this would be in conformity with provisions of the standard since it recognises postponement of r

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) With respect to revenue recognition under the standard, an item of income
shall not be considered material if it does not exceed one per cent of the
total income of the AIFI if the income is reckoned on a gross basis or one
per cent of the net profit (before taxes) if the income is reckoned net of
costs.
requirements.object: it does not exceed one per cent of the total income of the AIFI if the income is reckoned on a gross basis or one per cent o

conditions:
If any item of income is not considered to be material as per the above
norms
requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) If any item of income is not considered to be material as per the above
norms, it may be recognised when received.

The
issues that arise in this context have been identified and an AIFI may be guided
by the following while complying with the provisions of the standard.
requirements.object: following while complying with the provisions of the standard

requirements.full_sentence:
Since the enterprises are required to record the transactions
at the date of the occurrence thereof, the weekly average closing rate of the
preceding week can be used for recording the transactions occurring in the
relevant week, if the same approximates the actual rate at the date of the
transaction.
requirements.object: at the date of the occurrence thereof, the weekly average closing rate of the preceding week can be used for recording the transactions occurring in the relevant week

requirements.full_sentence:
In view of the practical difficulties in applying the exchange rates at
the dates of the transactions and since the standard allows the use of a rate that
approximates the actual rate at the date of the transaction, an AIFI may use
average rates as detailed below:
requirements.object: detailed below:

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Those foreign currency transactions, which are currently not being recorded
in Indian Rupees at the date of the transaction or are being recorded using a
notional exchange rate may now be recorded at the date of the transaction
by using the weekly average closing rate of the preceding week, published
by FEDAI, if the same approximates the actual rate at the date of the
transaction.
requirements.object: the date of the transaction by using the weekly average closing rate of the preceding week, published by FEDAI

requirements.full_sentence:
(d) If the weekly average closing rate of the preceding week does not
approximate the actual rate at the date of the transaction, the closing rate at
<image>
the date of the transaction shall be used.

conditions: if there are significant exchange fluctuations during the quarter
requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of non-integral foreign operations,
if there are significant exchange fluctuations during the quarter, the income
and expense items of non-integral foreign operations shall be translated by
using the exchange rate at the date of the transaction instead of the
quarterly average closing rate.
requirements.object: the exchange rate at the date of the transaction instead of the quarterly average closing rate

requirements.full_sentence:
To ensure uniformity among AIFIs, closing rate to be applied
for the purposes of AS 11 (revised 2003) for the relevant accounting period shall
be the last closing spot rate of exchange announced by FEDAI for that accounting
period.
requirements.object: spot rate of exchange announced by FEDAI for that accounting period

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Foreign Currency Translation Reserve (FCTR)
In the context of recognition of gains in profit and loss account from FCTR on
repatriation of accumulated profits / retained earnings from overseas branch(es),
it is clarified that the repatriation of accumulated profits shall not be considered as
disposal or partial disposal of interest in non-integral foreign operations as per AS
requirements.object: or partial disposal of interest in non-integral foreign operations as per AS

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, an AIFI shall not recognise in the profit and loss account the
<image>
proportionate exchange gains or losses held in the foreign currency translation
reserve on repatriation of profits from overseas operations.
requirements.object: and loss account the <image> proportionate exchange gains or losses held in the foreign currency translation reserve on repatr

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The Standard requires that an investment in an associate shall be
accounted for in consolidated financial statements under the equity method
subject to certain exceptions.
requirements.object: consolidated financial statements under the equity method subject to certain exceptions

requirements.full_sentence: Such an influence may be gained by share ownership, statute or agreement.
requirements.object: ownership, statute or agreement

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) From the above it is clear that though an AIFI may acquire more than 20 per
cent of voting power in the borrower entity in satisfaction of its advances it
may be able to demonstrate that it does not have the power to exercise
significant influence since the rights exercised by it are protective in nature
and not participative.
requirements.object: per cent of voting power in the borrower entity in satisfaction of its advances it may be able to demonstrate that it does not ha

requirements.full_sentence:
In such a circumstance, such investment may not be
treated as investment in associate under this Accounting Standard.
requirements.object: in associate under this Accounting Standard

requirements.full_sentence:
Hence
the test shall not be merely the proportion of investment but the intention to
acquire the power to exercise significant influence.
requirements.object: of investment but the intention to acquire the power to exercise significant influence

requirements.full_sentence:
With respect
to computer software which has been customised for an AIFI's use and is
expected to be in use for some time, the detailed recognition and
amortisation principle in respect of computer software prescribed in the
standard adequately addresses these issues and may be followed by it.

conditions: In case of jointly controlled entities

where an AIFI is required to present
consolidated financial statements
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of jointly controlled entities, where an AIFI is required to present
consolidated financial statements, the investment in joint ventures shall be
accounted for as per provisions of this standard.
requirements.object: per provisions of this standard

requirements.full_sentence:
It is
clarified that the standard shall not apply to investments, inventories and
financial assets such as loans and advances and shall generally be
applicable in so far as it relates to fixed assets.
requirements.object: and financial assets such as loans and advances and shall generally be applicable in so far as it relates to fixed a

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The standard shall generally apply to financial lease assets, and assets
acquired in settlement of claims only when the indications of impairment of
the entity are evident.
requirements.object: lease assets, and assets acquired in settlement of claims only when the indications of impairment of the entity are evide

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall disclose the information specified in this chapter in the notes to
accounts of the financial statements.
requirements.object: in this chapter in the notes to accounts of the financial statements

requirements.full_sentence:
The items listed in these Directions shall be disclosed in the 'Notes to Accounts'
to both solo level financial statements and CFS.
requirements.object: 'Notes to Accounts' to both solo level financial statements and CFS

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall make additional
disclosures where material.
requirements.object: material

requirements.full_sentence:
Unless specifically indicated, the prudential items
pertaining to subsidiaries shall be consolidated for the purpose of disclosure in
the notes to accounts as shown in its books of accounts / financial statements /
notes to accounts (without any adjustments to align them with the prudential
norms applicable to the AIFI).
requirements.object: purpose of disclosure in the notes to accounts as shown in its books of accounts / financial statements / notes to accounts (w

A summary of 'Significant Accounting Policies' and 'Notes to Accounts' shall be
shown separately.

In addition to the detailed schedules to the balance sheet, an AIFI is required to
furnish the following information in 'Notes to Accounts':
(1) Capital adequacy*
(Amount in ₹ crores) Sr.

Note -
*For CFS, the risk weighted assets of the subsidiaries shall be notionally recomputed applying the RBI directions applicable to
the AIFI.
requirements.object: the RBI directions applicable to the AIFI

Accounts' in the Annual Financial Statements.

as part of a strategic debt restructuring which are exempt from CME limits.

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.

For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of
these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
